title: Share a File with a Link
summary: Send a file to someone using a shareable link instead of an attachment.
learnerPromise: You will be able to create a shareable link for a file and send it to someone so they can view or download it.
whyItMatters: Shareable links let you send large files that might not fit in an email. You can also control who sees the file and turn off access later.
realLifeUses: A video file is too large to send as an email attachment
You want several people to view the same document without sending multiple copies
You need to collect feedback on a presentation from classmates or coworkers
You want someone to download a file without needing to create an account
materialsNeeded: A phone, tablet, or computer with internet access
A file uploaded to a cloud storage or file-sharing service
result: A shareable link copied to your clipboard, ready to paste into a message or email.
youWillLearn: How to find the share option for a file in cloud storage
How to set link permissions so others can view or edit
How to copy and send the link through a message or email
How to turn off or change sharing permissions when you no longer want the file shared
miniChallenge: Upload one file to cloud storage, create a shareable link, and send it to yourself in a message.
steps: Open your cloud storage app or website and locate the file you want to share
Tap or click the share icon next to the file
Choose the option to create a shareable link
Set the permission level — usually 'view only' is the safest starting choice
Copy the link and paste it into a message, email, or chat to send it
completionCriteria: You created a working shareable link for a file
You successfully sent the link and can open the file from it
commonProblems: The link does not work for the recipient — check that sharing permissions allow them to access it
The file is still private — look for a setting that says 'anyone with the link'
Forgot who you shared the file with — most services show a list of shared files and active links
tips: Use 'view only' links unless you want the other person to make changes
Set an expiration date on the link if your service offers that option
Avoid sharing links to sensitive personal documents publicly
You can turn off a link later if you no longer want people to access the file
